plausible_hypotheses.summary: Open a mixed-beta diagonal butterfly at anchor7, then hand control back to the untouched 8→9→10→11 relay backbone. This is earlier than the anchor8 entry-boundary family and geometrically different from the anchor9 butterfly family.

Use a tiny anchor7 x/y closed pair to shape the -Y→-Z corridor one step before the entry boundary. Listed as a plausible family, but not spent in this batch because H1 is the stronger geometric version of the same corridor idea.

Inject a small legal z-family seed at anchor5, then keep the best relay backbone untouched. This probes a far-field z-observability seed that is outside the late10/11, relay, and entry-boundary neighborhoods.
Chain a far anchor5 seed and an anchor7 corridor conditioner before the relay core. Left untested in this pass because H1/H3 must first show isolated value before spending budget on a coupled chain.

tested_hypotheses.summary: Open a mixed-beta diagonal butterfly at anchor7, then hand control back to the untouched 8→9→10→11 relay backbone. This is earlier than the anchor8 entry-boundary family and geometrically different from the anchor9 butterfly family.

Inject a small legal z-family seed at anchor5, then keep the best relay backbone untouched. This probes a far-field z-observability seed that is outside the late10/11, relay, and entry-boundary neighborhoods.

kf36_recheck.reason: Triggered because zseed_l5_neg4_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2 landed at 9.506 / 1.041 / 99.607 with frontier-adjacent max / mean, so it was close enough to justify a KF36 stability check.

kf36_rows.note: best max-priority candidate in this batch

bottom_line.scientific_conclusion: Yes — this batch found a stronger new direction, and it is the **anchor5 far z-seed relay family** rather than the anchor7 corridor-diagonal family. The family landed two useful frontier points: best-mean `zseed_l5_neg2_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2` = 9.302 / 1.130 / 99.617, and best-max `zseed_l5_neg4_plus_relaymax_unified_l9y2` = 9.506 / 1.041 / 99.607. Relative to `relaymax_unified_l9y2`, the best-mean point gains Δmean +0.211 and Δmax +0.013, while the best-max point gains Δmean +0.007 and Δmax +0.023. Relative to `entryrelay_l8x1_l9y1_unifiedcore`, both far-z-seed points still give back a few hundredths on max, so this is not the new overall max-frontier leader — but it is a real new family that cleanly improves the current relay branch under the same legality and continuity constraints.
